title: dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. haematodes
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. haematodes, also known as the purple marsh orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers and distinctive growth habits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to be beneficial in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. haematodes are said to have several health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving circulation, and acting as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
